extra.reasoning:
The property (monotonicity in chest_pain) is VIOLATED. chest_pain appears only in r2's conjunctive condition, and r2 (priority 100, the highest) sets acuity to self_care, the LOWEST acuity in the order [self_care, routine, urgent, emergent].

Counterexample, comparing the baseline (chest_pain=false) against flipping chest_pain=true with all other inputs held fixed at: confusion=T, hemoptysis=T, back_pain=T, severe_headache=T, slurred_speech=T, rash=T, dizziness=T, focal_weakness=F.

With chest_pain=FALSE: r2 does not fire (its conjunction requires chest_pain). The firing rules include r3 (hemoptysis -> emergent, prio 3), r5 (severe_headache -> emergent, prio 3), r6 (rash -> emergent, prio 3), plus several urgent/priority-2 rules. Highest priority among firing rules is 3, all emergent => acuity = emergent.

With chest_pain=TRUE (all else unchanged): all eight conjuncts of r2 are now satisfied, so r2 fires at priority 100, beating every other rule => acuity = self_care.

Setting chest_pain true thus drops acuity from emergent down to self_care, which is strictly lower in the enum order. The witness_assignment given is the chest_pain=true input that produces self_care; its chest_pain=false counterpart produces emergent, demonstrating the violation.
